about.quote: From the street to the studio, Hize reinterprets the foundations of graffiti beyond the wall, stripping away noise to reveal movement, rhythm and urban memory.
about.title: Translating the kinetic energy of the street into disciplined contemplation.
about.seoTitle: About Hize | Manuel Forner, Spanish Urban Artist
paragraphs: Manuel Forner "Hize" is a Spanish visual artist whose work transforms the language of traditional graffiti into abstract contemporary compositions. With a trajectory spanning over 25 years, his evolution reflects a continuous refinement of raw urban energy into sophisticated, gallery-ready forms.
Born from more than 30 years of painting in the streets, his practice explores the hidden structures between letters, the rhythm of movement, and the visual energy of urban culture.

Before fully developing his studio practice, Hize built a parallel career in dance and entertainment, working internationally as a breakdancer, performer and creative director. This connection between body, rhythm and gesture became a central part of his artistic language.
about.portraitAlt: Portrait of Spanish urban artist Manuel Forner Hize

vrArt.seoTitle: VR Art by Hize | Digital Graffiti Experiences
vrArt.heroTitle: VR Art is the digital extension of Hize's graffiti language - transforming gesture, movement and rhythm into immersive visual experiences.
vrArt.introLead: It is not merely a translation of physical paint to a digital canvas. It is the liberation of the mark from gravity and surface, using movement, gesture and spatial drawing as live visual expression.
vrArt.introText: This practice bridges the visceral, kinetic energy of street art with immersive digital tools, resulting in environments that are simultaneously raw and meticulously engineered.

disciplines.text: Audiences witness the genesis of form and colour in motion, projected or experienced through immersive formats.

process.alt: Spanish urban artist Hize painting large scale mural
process.lead: Each mural is developed from the identity of the space, combining Hize's graffiti roots with abstract composition, colour and movement. The execution balances street-level kinetic energy with the disciplined restraint of a gallery installation.
process.title: The authority of the avant-garde applied to structural space.
